MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard D. Dumont, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Scott Co. of California
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Eggleton, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Scott Co. of California's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Eggleton was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Cohen's declaration are sustained. Two orders re: ruling on granting the motion, and ruling on defendant's objections to evidence were signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Getinge USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Eggleton was exposed to asbestos-containing oven insulation attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' supplemental responses to defendant's special interrogatories, Mr. Schluntz's deposition testimony and defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Schluntz's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Eggleton was exposed to asbestos-containing oven insulation attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Schluntz's declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and punitive damages, which were the only issues that defendant sought to be summarily adjudicated. Off the record an order was signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
